title: Steve Kerr explains how the Celtics went from an ‘afterthought’ to a ‘real favorite’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Steve Kerr praised the Boston Celtics for becoming a top Eastern Conference team without Jayson Tatum before his return.
The Celtics swept the Golden State Warriors in the regular-season series, highlighted by a 120-99 victory at TD Garden.
Kerr credits the Celtics' success to playing younger players during Tatum's absence and maintaining strong team spacing and execution.

title: Advocates sue Worcester over response to mental health 911 calls, alleging discrimination
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Advocates filed a federal lawsuit against Worcester over its response to mental health 911 calls using armed police.
Plaintiffs claim Worcester sends armed officers to all mental health calls, violating federal anti-discrimination laws.
Less than 25% of Worcester police have completed crisis intervention training, according to the lawsuit.

title: Hilary Duff will be Northeastern University’s 2026 commencement speaker
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Hilary Duff announced as Northeastern University's 2026 commencement speaker in a Y2K-themed social media post.
The 2026 undergraduate commencement ceremony will take place at Fenway Park on April 29.
Northeastern's mascot starred in an Instagram video to announce Duff's appearance, featuring her song 'What Dreams Are Made Of.'

title: 3 roster needs Patriots must still address after free-agency spending spree
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots seek a proven No. 1 wide receiver, with A.J. Brown as a potential trade target.
New England aims to bolster pass rush after losing K'Lavon Chaisson; Draft may provide edge rusher options.
Depth at offensive tackle needed; Patriots might draft a successor for aging right tackle Morgan Moses.

title: Needham 16-year-old charged with murder in Roxbury slaying of woman, 61
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A 16-year-old from Needham has been charged with the murder of Marie Molea-Anthony, 61, in Roxbury last October.
The teen, whose identity is withheld, also faces charges of aggravated assault and unlawful possession of a firearm.
Boston police are investigating the incident and encourage those with information to contact them or submit anonymous tips.

title: Chelsea man released decades after murder conviction won’t be retried, prosecutors say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Thomas Rosa Jr., 64, will not be retried after spending 34 years in prison for a 1985 murder.
The Suffolk County DA dropped charges, citing new DNA evidence and changes in forensic testing and case law.
Rosa becomes the 100th exoneree from Massachusetts since 1989 after maintaining innocence throughout his imprisonment.

title: Former Attleboro man gets 35-40 years in prison for raping 5-year-old niece
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: William Machado, 35, was sentenced to 35-40 years for raping his 5-year-old niece in Attleboro, Massachusetts.
Machado was convicted on multiple charges including aggravated child rape and lewd acts with a child under 16.
Machado moved to Chicago post-crime; extradited back to Massachusetts after police tracked him through financial transactions.

title: A month after tragic shooting, Colin Dorgan, Blackstone Valley hockey triumph to win R.I. state title
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Blackstone Valley hockey team won the R.I. Division II state title with a 3-2 victory over Lincoln in quadruple overtime.
Senior Colin Dorgan scored the equalizing goal for Blackstone, playing in memory of his three family members killed in a shooting.
The team wore jerseys with initials of the deceased, signifying their support and tribute throughout their championship run.

title: Bruins call up former 1st-round pick Lukas Reichel on emergency basis
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins recalled winger Lukas Reichel from Providence on an emergency basis ahead of their game against the Winnipeg Jets.
Reichel was acquired from the Vancouver Canucks for a 2026 sixth-round pick just before the NHL trade deadline.
In three games with Providence, Reichel recorded one goal and four assists, showcasing his potential to boost Bruins' offense.

title: World Cup games at Gillette no longer in jeopardy after Foxborough officially approves license
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Foxborough approved an event license for seven World Cup games at Gillette Stadium after resolving security funding issues.
The Kraft Sports & Entertainment Group and Boston Soccer 2026 agreed to cover $7.8 million in security costs.
A traffic management plan, approved by the Massachusetts Department of Transportation, supports 21,000 patrons per game.
